description:
Introduce a single materializer fn that owns the descr→stmts vocabulary.

## Shape

```rust
fn descr_to_materialize(
    d: &Descr,
    m: &mut Module,
    fn_idx: usize,
    bid: BlockId,
) -> Option<Var>
```

Returns a fresh Var bound to the literal value, after pushing the necessary stmts into the block. None if d isn't materializable.

First arm: scalars (delegates to existing literal_to_const → Const). No new capabilities yet.

## Wire up

Route all four current `literal_to_const` call sites through `descr_to_materialize`:
- src/ir_reducer.rs:130 (Term::Call rewrite)
- src/ir_reducer.rs:148 (Term::TailCall rewrite)
- src/ir_reducer.rs:174 (Term::Call → Term::Return)
- src/ir_reducer.rs:197 (Term::Return rewrite)

Each call site shrinks: instead of `literal_to_const` + `fresh_var` + `Stmt::Let(Const)` push, just `descr_to_materialize(...)?` returns the Var.

## What to verify

- No behavior change. All existing fixtures + tests pass byte-identical.
- `literal_to_const` becomes a private helper of the materializer (or stays as-is, called from inside).
- `is_scalar_literal` unchanged in this ticket — Arc .2 widens it.

## Blocks

fz-f88.2 (closure arm), fz-f88.3 (tuple/empty-list arms).
title: fz-RS.1 — descr_to_materialize() scaffold, scalar arm only
